safety_policies.title: Prohibited Items
safety_policies.summary: Items not permitted inside the stadium.
details: Outside food and beverages (sealed water bottles ≤500ml permitted)
Glass containers
Weapons of any kind
Drones or unmanned aerial vehicles
Laser pointers
Large bags (must be clear bag ≤12x6x12 inches or small clutch ≤4.5x6.5 inches)
Professional cameras with detachable lenses >6 inches
Fireworks or pyrotechnics
Flagpoles longer than 30cm
Animals (except certified service animals with documentation)

safety_policies.title: Emergency Evacuation Procedure
safety_policies.summary: What to do in case of an evacuation order.
details: Listen for the official PA evacuation announcement (three long tones + voice instruction).
Follow illuminated green EXIT signs to your nearest exit.
Do not use elevators during fire evacuation — use stairs.
Accessible evacuation: proceed to the nearest Evacuation Refuge Area (ERA) marked with blue wheelchair sign; trained volunteers will assist.
Do not return to collect belongings.
Assembly point: North Plaza (Gate 1 exterior) or South Parking Lot (Gate 3 exterior).
Emergency services number: 911.

safety_policies.title: Accessible Services Policy
safety_policies.summary: Services available for fans with disabilities.
details: Complimentary personal care assistant (PCA) ticket with any accessible seating purchase.
Assistive listening devices available at Guest Services (Gate 1 and Gate 3).
Sign-language interpretation available for PA announcements — request at Gate 1 Guest Services.
Sensory kits (ear defenders, fidget tools) available at Family Zone (Gate 4).
Companion restrooms available at all four gate concourses.
Wheelchair loan service at Gate 1 Guest Services (first-come, first-served, ID required).

safety_policies.title: Fan Code of Conduct
safety_policies.summary: Expected behavior for all match attendees.
details: Respect all other fans, staff, and players regardless of nationality, race, religion, gender, or disability.
Discriminatory chanting or gestures will result in immediate ejection.
No standing in seating areas unless in designated standing sections.
No excessive alcohol consumption — security may refuse entry to visibly intoxicated individuals.
Photography for personal use is permitted; commercial use requires press accreditation.

safety_policies.title: Lost & Found
safety_policies.summary: How to report and retrieve lost items.
details: Lost & Found office located at Gate 1 Guest Services, open T-3h to +3h post-match.
Lost children: proceed immediately to nearest security post; child will be taken to Family Reunion Point at Gate 4 Guest Services.
Lost items after the event: call +1-800-STADIUM or email lostandfound@metroarena2026.com within 30 days.

safety_policies.title: Medical Emergency
safety_policies.summary: Steps to take if you or someone near you has a medical emergency.
details: For life-threatening emergencies: call 911 immediately.
Alert the nearest steward or security officer — all are first-aid trained.
AED (Automated External Defibrillator) units are located at every gate concourse and food court.
Nearest medical station: Gate 1 (North) or Gate 3 (South).
Do not move an injured person unless there is immediate danger of further harm.

faqs.question: What time do gates open?
faqs.answer: Gates 1, 2, and 4 open 3 hours before kick-off. Gate 3 opens 2.5 hours before kick-off. Arrival at least 90 minutes before kick-off is recommended to avoid queues.

faqs.question: Is there free Wi-Fi in the stadium?
faqs.answer: Yes. Connect to 'MetroArena_Guest' — no password required. The network is stadium-wide and supports up to 80,000 simultaneous connections.

faqs.question: Where can I charge my phone?
faqs.answer: Charging stations are available at each food court and at Guest Services desks at Gates 1 and 3. USB-A, USB-C, and wireless (Qi) pads available.

faqs.question: Can I re-enter if I leave the stadium?
faqs.answer: Re-entry is permitted up to 30 minutes after kick-off with your original ticket. After that, re-entry is not allowed.

faqs.question: Where is the family sensory room?
faqs.answer: The sensory-friendly room is in the West Family Zone (Gate 4), Room W-10. It has low lighting, sound dampening, and dedicated staff.

faqs.question: What languages does stadium staff speak?
faqs.answer: Staff at information desks speak English, Spanish, French, Arabic, Portuguese, and Mandarin. Translation cards are available for 20+ additional languages.

faqs.question: Are there vegetarian or halal food options?
faqs.answer: Yes. Vegan Corner (North Food Court) is fully vegan/vegetarian. Halal Grill (North Food Court) and Tacos & More (South Food Court) offer halal-certified options. Allergen menus available at every vendor.

faqs.question: How do I get to the stadium by public transit?
faqs.answer: The Blue Line (North Station, 3-min walk to Gate 1) and Red Line (East Station, 5-min walk to Gate 2) run every 4–5 minutes on match day. Green Line serves Gate 3 (South Station, 4-min walk). Purple Line serves Gate 4 (West Station, 6-min walk).

faqs.question: What should I do if I lose my child?
faqs.answer: Immediately alert the nearest steward. Your child will be taken to the Family Reunion Point at Gate 4 Guest Services. Stadium-wide PA announcements can be requested.

faqs.question: Is smoking allowed?
faqs.answer: Smoking and vaping are prohibited inside the stadium and within 50 meters of all entrances. Designated smoking areas are in external plazas — ask a steward for the nearest one.

sustainability_bins.label: Landfill / General Waste
accepts: mixed waste not accepted by other bins
